items.title: After treatment delay at Tumpat Hospital goes viral, Kelantan health dept offers explanation
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 30 — The Kelantan Health Department will conduct a further investigation and improve operating procedures at the Tumpat Hospital Emergency and Trauma Unit following a treatment delay issue that went viral on social media yesterday. The state health department in a statement yesterday, informed that it noted the viral post uploaded yesterday regarding the treatment of a 17-year-old female patient who sought medical attention at the hospital. According to the statement, the patient arrived at the Tumpat Hospital Emergency and Trauma Unit at 9:50pm last Wednesday, complaining of shortness of breath, fever, cough, and a cold.

items.title: Penang cops consider joint school inspections amid rise in drug-laced vape cases, says state police chief
items.summary: According to the New Straits Times , state police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the proposal was discussed during a State Drug Eradication Action Council meeting chaired by Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow on April 6. Azizee said police had not received any reports of drug-laced vape devices being used in schools but stressed that enforcement efforts were ongoing. As part of the state’s crime prevention efforts, each school has been assigned two police officers tasked with strengthening disciplinary monitoring and crime prevention measures.

items.title: Body of jet ski operator found off Langkawi coast
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 30 — The body of a man who was feared drowned after he was believed to have tried to save a drifting jet ski in an incident off Jalan Pantai Tengah, Langkawi yesterday, was found today. Langkawi Zone 4 Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) chief Superintendent Mohd Zamri Abdul Ghani said the body of 23-year-old Arash Nashran Abdul Maser, the jet ski operator, was discovered at about 6 pm by a search and rescue team conducting patrols at sea. At 6 pm, the victim’s body was found and brought to the jetty by boat,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Permanent cultural house strengthens Dusun Labuk identity, visibility
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Dusun Labuk community is gaining greater visibility and recognition with the establishment of a permanent cultural house at Koisaan Cultural Village (KCV) in Hongkod Koisaan, Penampang. Community leader Janai @ Tuni Sikawa @ Sikua said the new facility allows visitors to experience the Dusun Labuk way of life through interactive cultural activities and exhibitions. "We will also showcase Magalai Labuk traditional dance performances, traditional musical instruments and Sabung Labuk traditional attire known for its colourful bunga gosing embroidery.

items.title: Defence minister: US considering selling missiles to Malaysia after Norway missile delivery falls through
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 30 — The United States (US) has assured Malaysia that it will consider selling missile as a solution to the issues facing the country regarding the acquisition of the weapon system from Norway, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He said the matter was raised by US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth in a bilateral meeting held on the sidelines of the 23rd IISS Shangri-La Dialogue (SLD) here which took place from yesterday to Sunday. He said he also briefed Hegseth on the issues facing Malaysia regarding the purchase of Naval Strike Missile (NSM) from Norway and sought the US's views on the matter.

items.title: Rats, dirty kitchens and poor food handling shut five Puchong eateries
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Five eateries in Puchong have been ordered to close temporarily after failing hygiene inspections that uncovered rat droppings, improper food storage and poor food handling practices, according to a report by national daily The Star . The Subang Jaya City Council (MBSJ) said the outlets were among 15 eateries inspected during a joint enforcement operation in Taman Perindustrian Puchong Utama earlier this week. According to MBSJ, inspectors found a range of hygiene-related offences, including the presence of rat droppings, unsatisfactory food storage practices, substandard food handling and a failure to maintain pest control records or implement pest management measures.

items.title: #NSTviral: Who gets priority on a jogging track? KLCC Park debate heats up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A heated rant over people walking on the jogging track at KLCC Park has sparked debate on Threads. The Threads user, in the post, criticised the family's action, drawing backlash from other social media users. However, the post also opened a wider debate on whether walkers should avoid using jogging tracks in public parks, especially when separate footpaths are available.

items.title: Woman breaks down after Butterworth court grants RM240,000 bail in forced labour trafficking case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — A couple accused of trafficking Indonesian women for forced labour pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court in Butterworth yesterday and were granted bail totalling RM240,000. National daily Utusan Malaysia reported that Chan Koon Pheng, 40, and her husband, Tan Kok Teong, 42, entered their pleas after the charges were read before Judge Ooi Sheow Yean. Utusan Malaysia said that in separate proceedings before the same court, Chan also pleaded not guilty to four additional charges of a similar nature, including two before Judge Roslan Hamid.

items.title: Unrest outside US immigration detention centre, 9 arrested
items.summary: NEWARK: Protesters clashed with US law enforcement outside an immigration detention centre in New Jersey, a top US official said Friday, after inmates inside staged a hunger strike over conditions. US Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin posted on X that on Thursday evening, "approximately 100 anti-ICE rioters gathered around the Delaney Hall ICE facility. He said nine people were arrested.

items.title: WHO chief meets health, humanitarian partners in DR Congo as Ebola cases mount
items.summary: KIGALI, (Rwanda): At least 134 confirmed cases of Ebola have been reported in the current outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, according to an update Friday from the World Health Organisation (WHO), Anadolu Ajansi reported. The report indicated the confirmed cases included nine in Uganda, and a doctor from the US, who had treated patients in Congo and is currently receiving care in Germany. Deaths from Ebola stood at 18 among the confirmed cases in the two countries, the report showed, as WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus met health and humanitarian partners operating in Congo to discuss the response to the outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus in the country's east.

items.title: Trump says making final decision on Iran deal
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said that he was making his decision on a potential deal with Iran, though Teheran insisted there was still "no final agreement" on ending the Middle East war. A report from Iran's Fars news agency also rebutted several key elements of Trump's characterisation of the deal, citing informed sources as calling his remarks a "mixture of truth and lies." Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei, however, told state media that the Islamic republic "said goodbye to the language of 'must' 47 years ago."

items.title: Vincent Tan seeks buyer for 30pct Prudential Malaysia stake [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Group founder Tan Sri Vincent Tan is exploring a sale of his remaining 30 per cent stake in Prudential Assurance Malaysia Bhd (Prudential Malaysia), in a deal that could rank among the largest insurance transactions in Malaysia in recent years, people familiar with the matter said. The two parties resolved a long-running legal dispute that paved the way for Prudential's wholly owned subsidiary, Prudential Corp Holdings Ltd (PCHL), to increase its stake in Prudential Malaysia from 51 per cent to the maximum permitted 70 per cent. Prudential Malaysia is one of the country's largest life insurers and forms part of Prudential Plc's Malaysian life insurance segment, alongside its stake in Prudential BSN Takaful Bhd, one of Malaysia's leading takaful operators.

items.title: IMF, World Bank, IEA chiefs warn of summer fuel scarcity if Hormuz strait remains closed
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The heads of the International Monetary Fund, World Bank and International Energy Agency warned on Friday of the risks to fuel security during peak demand summer months if oil shipping through the Strait of Hormuz does not return to normal. "Global oil inventories are being drawn down at a record pace in response to the major loss of supply through the Strait of Hormuz," the heads of the agencies said in a joint statement. "If shipping flows do not return to normal, continued rapid depletion of global oil inventories ahead of peak summer oil demand in the Northern Hemisphere would present increasing risks for fuel security, market conditions, and broader economic resilience."

items.title: 'Umno holds edge in possible state polls'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno's confidence ahead of rumoured state elections may be valid due to the Bersatu-Pas fracture and the launch of Bersama by former PKR members Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, say analysts. Merdeka Centre co-founder and programmes director Ibrahim Suffian said current circumstances could drive the party's momentum ahead of the possible Melaka and Johor polls. The analysts said the rumoured state elections could see Umno secure a majority of seats in the state assembly.

items.title: Total vape ban may fuel black market, warns criminologist
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The use of "mushroom", "zombie" and "kuah" vape products among schoolchildren should be treated as a serious public health and criminological issue rather than dismissed as just moral panic. Senior criminologist Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy said the trend reflected a broader shift in youth risk behaviour, illicit markets and digital consumer culture. The Asian Criminological Society board member said the growing attraction to stronger vape substances among minors showed how deviant subcultures could evolve rapidly when demand, peer influence, curiosity and weak regulation intersected.

items.title: Police, schools may jointly carry out checks in classes for vapes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang police are considering working with schools to jointly check students' belongings for vape products to keep them out of the children's hands. This, he said, included engagement sessions with local communities as well as state Education Department officials on the dangers of synthetic drug-laced vape products. Police conducted Op Vape Bil 1/2026 with several agencies across Penang on April 13-17.

items.title: Israel PM says forces pushing into Lebanon as delegations meet in US
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Friday that his country's forces had pushed deeper inside Lebanon, even as Israeli and Lebanese military delegations were holding landmark security talks in Washington. Israel also kept up its heavy bombardment of south Lebanon on Friday, with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun emphasising in a call with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio "the need to exert all efforts to reach a ceasefire" as an essential first step, his office said. 'VERY DIFFICULT' This week, Israel said it would ramp up operations in Lebanon, including on the ground in the south.

items.title: Teachers need help to clamp down on vape menace
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Vaping has become a full-blown crisis in schools, with nearly 20,000 student cases recorded nationwide in 2024 alone. Parent Action Group for Education chairman Datin Noor Azimah Abdul Rahim said the alarming figures, based on Education Ministry data presented in Parliament, reflected how deeply vaping had penetrated schools. She said concerns over youth vaping were particularly acute in Johor, Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu and Perlis, where authorities had either debated or moved to restrict vape sales amid growing public concern.

items.title: Drug-laced vape use mushrooming [WATCH]
items.summary: He claimed that students were moving beyond conventional fruit-flavoured vape products in their search of more intense highs. Subbarow also warned that the latest vape products entering the underground market were becoming increasingly dangerous, with some allegedly containing drugs or unidentified substances. "Vape products are packaged and marketed in ways that appeal to children.

items.title: Sudoku pioneer Yoshinao Anpuku sees Malaysian players gaining skills beyond number grids
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — After spending years watching Sudoku players race through number grids, Yoshinao Anpuku now hopes more of them will begin looking beyond Sudoku itself. The president of Tokyo-based Nikoli, the Japanese puzzle publisher widely associated with popularising Sudoku worldwide, said players who explore other forms of logic puzzles may develop stronger problem-solving instincts that eventually improve their Sudoku skills as well. Still, Anpuku said he hopes Sudoku players will gradually explore a wider variety of logic puzzles beyond the familiar number grid.

items.title: Ex-Trump attorney-general Pam Bondi defends Epstein files release
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Former US attorney-general Pam Bondi on Friday defended the Justice Department's handling of its files on sex trafficker Jeffrey Epstein, while acknowledging "redaction errors" in a release that critics say shielded key information while identifying alleged victims. Bondi was giving evidence to the House Oversight Committee in a transcribed interview rather than a sworn, videotaped deposition, frustrating Democrats and survivors who accuse President Donald Trump's administration of withholding the full record of the disgraced financier's sex trafficking case. "To the best of my knowledge, the Department produced everything required under the Epstein Files Transparency Act," Bondi said in the statement.

items.title: In China, AMD CEO Lisa Su is understated but Nvidia's Huang more razzmatazz
items.summary: Just a year ago at the annual Computex trade show in Taipei, Huang said Nvidia's market share in China had dropped to 50 per cent from 95 per cent due to US export controls. Huang has previously estimated that China's AI chip market would be worth US$50 billion this year. By contrast, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) commands four per cent of China's AI chip market, according to IDC, a research firm, and it has more routes into the country than AI accelerators, which are Nvidia's mainstay.

items.title: 'Jaslinda left a note', says mountain guide
items.summary: IPOH: An inscription left on the ground has become proof that missing female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, had reached the summit of Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah. A member of the Perak Forestry Mountain Guide (MGP) team, who wished to be known only as Hafiz, said he discovered the inscription when he arrived at the summit while serving as the rear guard, or 'sweeper', for the Trans Spencer Chapman hiking expedition. "She probably reached the summit of Gunung Batu Putih at about 10am.

items.title: Perlis Mufti: Shooting rampaging livestock permissible in Islam, halal to eat
items.summary: Perlis Mufti Datuk Dr Mohd Asri Zainul Abidin said using hunting dogs is also allowed in Islam. However, he said one must recite Bismillah (in the name of God) before releasing an arrow, firing a shot or releasing a hunting dog. A video went viral showing Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah firing a shot at a sacrificial cow that was running amok during a korban (Islamic slaughter rites) programme by the Kuala Perlis state constituency yesterday.

items.title: Flights, hotels divide travel into haves, have-nots
items.summary: Rising airfares and hotel rates are making budget-conscious Americans delay or cancel summer trips while wealthy travellers are holding fast to vacation plans despite higher prices. Overall, 45 per cent of Americans in a recent Deloitte survey said they already made travel plans for this summer, the lowest in the last six years. Still, he said travel was growing across income groups and leisure demand was "incredibly" strong.

items.title: AI chip workers are demanding more as company profits soar
items.summary: Runaway profits and sky-high valuations for microchip companies have fuelled worker demands over pay packages in South Korea, raising the question: who profits from the artificial intelligence (AI) boom? After memory chip giant Samsung Electronics reached a deal with its biggest union over bonuses, averting a major strike, AFP looks at what the dispute might mean for the industry. Samsung's value topped US$1 trillion this month, followed by South Korean rival SK hynix and US chipmaker Micron — newcomers to a previously exclusive club of around a dozen companies, nearly all American.

items.title: NST Leader: Sanctioning the death penalty
items.summary: Since Merdeka in 1957, Malaysia has executed 469 people. The death penalty has been the subject of much debate and even public fascination. The death penalty emerged independently across early ancient civilisations.

items.title: UN says protecting children online an 'urgent priority'
items.summary: GENEVA: Making the digital world safe for children is an urgent priority, the United Nations said on Friday, adding that those responsible for online harm must be held to account. UN rights chief Volker Türk said states had to force tech giants to embed child safety into their platforms, and said child harm was the direct result of business practices and design choices. "Blanket social media bans are not a one-off panacea," said Türk.

items.title: US judge freezes Trump 'slush fund' for allies
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A federal judge on Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration from moving ahead with a US$1.8 billion compensation package that critics have denounced as a "slush fund" for President Donald Trump's political allies. US District Judge Leonie Brinkema barred the administration from taking any further action to create or operate the fund while she considers whether to impose a longer-lasting pause. But opponents say the fund has no clear legal basis, little public oversight and could be used to reward loyalists, including defendants convicted of crimes related to the 2021 assault on the US Capitol by Trump supporters.

items.title: Body of jet ski operator found off Langkawi coast
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The body of a man who was feared drowned after he was believed to have tried to save a drifting jet ski in an incident off Jalan Pantai Tengah, Langkawi yesterday, was found today. Langkawi Zone 4 Fire and Rescue Department chief Mohd Zamri Abdul Ghani said the body of 23-year-old Arash Nashran Abdul Maser, the jet ski operator, was discovered at about 6pm by a search and rescue team conducting patrols at sea. At 6pm, the victim's body was found and brought to the jetty by boat," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang, Tengku Ampuan extend journalists' day greetings
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, and the Tengku Ampuan of Pahang, Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah, have extended National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 greetings to media practitioners and journalists across the country. In a post on the Kesultanan Pahang Facebook page, the royal couple expressed appreciation for the dedication and sacrifices of journalists committed to delivering accurate, credible and trustworthy information to the public. "Their Royal Highnesses hope that media practitioners will continue to uphold ethical, professional and responsible journalism in line with technological developments and the evolving challenges of today's communications landscape," the post read.

items.title: WFH fuel savings 'marginal' as petrol demand keeps rising, says economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petrol consumption continues to increase despite the government calling for work-from-home (WFH) arrangements last April, signalling the problem lies deeper beyond household behaviour. Centre for Future Studies Bhd chief executive officer and chief economist Dr Mohd Yusof Saari said that Malaysia's petrol demand is driven by logistics, production, household mobility and energy-intensive sectors. He added that private-sector workers, logistics operators, schools, small businesses, retailers and service providers still require physical mobility — groups not involved in the government's WFH policy.

items.title: Health Department investigates viral claim of delayed treatment at Tumpat Hospital
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The state Health Department is investigating a viral social media claim alleging that a 17-year-old patient at Tumpat Hospital was subjected to delayed treatment on Wednesday. Its director, Datuk Dr Mohd Azman Yacob, said the incident occurred at the hospital's Emergency and Trauma Unit. However, Dr Azman said that when hospital staff called the patient again for further examination at 12.02am, she was found to have left the hospital without informing staff.

items.title: Singaporean nurse killed in crash, falls from Johor Causeway flyover
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A 25-year-old Singaporean nurse was killed after his motorcycle crashed and he fell from a flyover along the Johor Causeway here yesterday. Johor Baru Selatan police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat said the victim was believed to have mistakenly entered the car lane before attempting to turn back against traffic. The victim died at the scene.

items.title: Tengku Hassanal calls on Muslims to strengthen ties, increase good deeds in Zulhijjah
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, has called on Muslims to strengthen ties, increase acts of worship and embrace the spirit of sacrifice for religion, race and country throughout the month of Zulhijjah. His Highness said Zulhijah is the best time for Muslims to increase prayers, 'zikir' (remembrance) and charitable deeds. Tengku Hassanal also called on Muslims to strengthen the spirit of sacrifice, particularly in easing disputes and misunderstandings among Muslims, especially within the Malay community, as expressed by the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, and the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, in their recent Aidiladha messages.

items.title: Firearms not meant to be discharged at will in Malaysia, says lawyer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Firearms are not meant to be discharged at will in Malaysia, even by licensed holders, lawyer Vivek Sukumaran said. Vivek said the incident, which has sparked public concern and is now under police investigation, underscored the distinction between possessing a firearm and lawfully using one. He said investigations should examine whether the conduct complied with all applicable legal requirements, including provisions under the Arms Act 1960.

items.title: MN Holdings's Q3 net profit jumps 29.6pct to RM21.39mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MN Holdings Bhd's net profit for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, rose 29.6 per cent to RM21.39 million from RM16.49 million a year earlier. Its revenue climbed 57 per cent to RM200.7 million, compared with RM127.42 million previously. For the nine-month period, net profit surged 88.5 per cent to RM68.15 million from RM36.15 million a year earlier, while revenue increased 84 per cent to RM657.72 million compared with RM356 million previously.

items.title: Will PM Anwar call for early elections? How political tensions and rising costs are fuelling speculation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Malaysia’s Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has said he would consider calling for snap polls if tensions within his ruling alliance got worse. Here’s why the South-east Asian nation could see early polls: Synchronising state and federal polls Malaysia is a federal system and most state polls are usually held alongside the federal election every five years. The Election Commission said in February an early general election would allow the state polls to be held concurrently, reducing costs, media reported.

items.title: Indonesian worker crushed to death by collapsed concrete structure in Bukit Mertajam
items.summary: The Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Movement Centre (PGO), in a statement, identified the victim as Marzuki, 27. It said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 3.55 pm, following which five personnel and a fire engine from the Penanti Fire and Rescue Station were dispatched to the scene. “The operations team then moved the victim to a safe area, but he was pronounced dead at the scene by Ministry of Health (MOH) medical personnel,” the statement said.

items.title: Johor BN sets June 14 convention to refine strategy, unity and direction
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 29 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) will hold its convention on June 14 as a platform to strengthen the coalition’s resolve, unity and direction at the state level. Johor Menteri Besar and the state BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the decision was made during the state BN meeting held today. In this regard, Onn Hafiz said Johor BN remains fully committed to strengthening cooperation with MyPPP to further enhance unity and streamline the coalition’s machinery throughout Johor.

items.title: Strong response to KL International Book Fair 2026 shows reading culture still alive, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed his appreciation for the extraordinary response from the people to the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair (KILF) 2026 which opened today. Anwar said the presence of various levels of society including parents, teachers, educators, students and children gave a clear signal that the culture of reading and gaining knowledge still has a special place in the soul of society. The Prime Minister said the main challenge of today’s society is no longer the issue of lack of information, but rather how to choose quality and beneficial knowledge.

items.title: NRD receives over 1,500 applications to shorten names since 2024
items.summary: MARANG, May 29 — The National Registration Department (NRD) has received 1,575 applications to shorten lengthy names on birth certificates since 2024. Its director-general, Datuk Badrul Hisham Alias, said of the total, 719 applications to shorten names were received in 2024, followed by 624 applications in 2025 and 232 applications recorded from January this year until today. He said that based on the amendments passed in Parliament, children born overseas to Malaysian mothers would automatically obtain Malaysian citizenship without the need to submit an application.

items.title: Strong winds, rough seas forecast for Sabah, Labuan and northern Sarawak from May 30 to June 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has forecast significant weather conditions in western Sabah, the Federal Territory of Labuan and northern Sarawak following the convergence of strong westerly winds expected from May 30 to June 1. It said the conditions could result in strong winds and rough seas, as well as the formation of squall lines expected to bring wetter and windier conditions over the affected areas and waters. “In this regard, MetMalaysia has issued a strong winds and rough seas warning for the South China Sea and the northern Straits of Melaka waters, effective until June 1,” it said in a statement today.

items.title: Anwar calls for responsible reporting in age of information overload
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has stressed the importance of responsible reporting in an era of information overload and borderless news dissemination to ensure facts are conveyed accurately, ethically and without undermining social harmony. In a Facebook post marking the National Journalists’ Day (Hawana) today, Anwar said the role of the media extends beyond reporting the news, carrying instead a major responsibility to uphold truth with integrity, credibility, courage and accountability. “Today, we commemorate National Journalists’ Day as a tribute to journalists and media practitioners who have long served as the voice of the people and as one of the key pillars strengthening the nation’s democracy.

items.title: Man found dead in locked car outside Penang ice cream shop
items.summary: Timur Laut District Deputy Police Chief Supt Lim Swee Sake said police received information on the incident from members of the public at 11.47am. “Investigations on the victim’s body and the surrounding area found no elements of foul play,” he said in a statement today. Lim said police estimated that the victim had died about five days before he was discovered.

items.title: Ampang, Sri Petaling LRT line runs on modified schedule after Chan Sow Lin derailment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Light Rail Transit (LRT) stations along the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line continued to operate today on a modified service schedule following the train derailment near Chan Sow Lin Station yesterday. A Bernama survey at several stations along the line, including from Cheras Station to Pudu Station, found that operations were running as usual with ticket counters open to the public. A passenger, Arif Norazlan, 21, said his journey from Cheras Station to Pudu Station went smoothly thanks to the assistance and guidance provided by Rapid Rail staff.

items.title: Ewon’s Upko applies to join GRS, moves to deepen Sabah coalition ties
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 29 — The United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) is officially asking to become a component party of the governing Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) coalition. “The ‘Sabah First’ vision championed by Upko is aligned with GRS’ struggle through the Sabah Maju Jaya 2.0 roadmap, which must be strengthened through closer cooperation and unity among Sabah local parties,” he added. GRS currently has five component parties — Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah (Gagasan Rakyat/PGRS), Parti Bersatu Sabah (PBS), Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah (PHRS), Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) and Parti Cinta Sabah (PCS) after the departure of several parties following the state election.

items.title: Kuala Pilah crash: Car skids, plunges onto house roof in Seri Menanti
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, May 29 — A car skidded out of control and ended up on the roof of a house in an unusual late-night accident near Seri Menanti. According to national news agency Bernama, the incident occurred along Jalan Kampung Tanjung Bachang, where the vehicle is believed to have lost control before plunging onto the rooftop of a home situated at the foot of a hill. Kuala Pilah District Police Chief Supt Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said two passengers sustained minor injuries and were treated at Kuala Pilah Hospital.

items.title: MyIMMs system disruption caused by internal technical issue, no cyberattack involved, says Home Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — The Ministry of Home Affairs (KDN) has assured that the temporary disruption involving the Malaysian Immigration System (MyIMMs) that affected inspection and processing operations at several entry points in the country today does not involve any cybersecurity incident. KDN in a statement today said the disruption was caused by an internal technical problem that affected the system’s operations for approximately three hours and 45 minutes before immediate restorations were implemented. “KDN would like to emphasise that the disruption that occurred did not involve any cybersecurity incident.

items.title: Kluang childcare teacher accused of forcing pupil to drink chilli-infused water
items.summary: KLUANG, May 29 — Police are investigating a claim that a seven-year-old child was forced to drink chilli-infused water by a teacher at a childcare centre in Taman Sri Kluang here. Kluang police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the child's guardian lodged a report at about 8.46 pm yesterday, alleging that the teacher had forced the child to consume the drink. Bahrin said the case is being investigated under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001 and urged anyone with information on the incident to contact the nearest police station or the Kluang district police headquarters at 07-7784222.

items.title: Immigration counters nationwide shut until June 3 for Agong’s birthday, Wesak
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — All Immigration Department (JIM) counters, including at Urban Transformation Centres (UTCs), will be closed on Monday (June 1) and Tuesday (June 2). It said in a social media post that the counters will be closed on Monday in conjunction with the Official Birthday of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and Wesak Day on Tuesday. Earlier, the government agreed to grant public holidays in conjunction with Wesak Day and the Official Birthday of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.

items.title: Govt opens tenders for battery energy storage projects nationwide to strengthen power grid
items.summary: KUCHING, May 29 — Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) projects are being expanded nationwide as the government moves to strengthen grid stability and support the growing use of renewable energy, particularly solar power. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said tenders for BESS projects have been opened at several locations in peninsular Malaysia following the increasing implementation of renewable energy initiatives such as solar. He said BESS plays a crucial role in maintaining grid stability as solar power depends on sunlight, while renewable energy sources that do not provide base load supply can affect the stability of the electricity system.

items.title: Foul smell leads to grisly discovery of decomposed body in rented Melaka shophouse, tenant’s son in custody as suspect
items.summary: MELAKA, May 29 — A decomposed body, believed to have been dead for between two weeks and a month, was found wrapped in a folded mattress inside a shophouse in Pokok Mangga here last night. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the body, whose identity has yet to be established, was discovered after the owner of the premises inspected the property when he was unable to contact the tenant. Upon entering the shophouse, the owner detected a foul smell and found a suspicious mattress bundle in the living room before alerting the police.

items.title: Private ambulance driver faces murder charge over death of two-year-old in Temerloh
items.summary: TEMERLOH, May 29 — A private ambulance driver was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a two-year-old boy. No plea was recorded from the accused, Muhammad Iqbal Fitri Mazlan, 29, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The charge was read out before Magistrate Anis Adlina Abu Johan.

items.title: Man charged in Sandakan with trafficking Malaysian teen and three foreigners for sex work
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 – A 32-year-old man was charged in the Sandakan Magistrate’s Court today with trafficking four people, including three minor girls, for sexual exploitation. Chu faces three charges of trafficking minors for sexual exploitation, involving two 15-year-olds—one a Malaysian and the other a foreigner—and a 17-year-old foreigner. The court denied bail for the accused and set June 15 for mention at the Sessions Court.

items.title: Anwar says will clarify MA63 negotiations and grant review in Sabah tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he will provide clarification on negotiations between the Federal Government and the Sabah government, including the review of Sabah’s 40 per cent special grant entitlement under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), tomorrow. “I will explain that tomorrow in Sabah. Yesterday, Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the federal government remains committed to correcting the previous administration’s official position, including undertaking a review of Sabah’s 40 per cent special grant entitlement.

items.title: ‘This is dangerous’: Anwar warns Malaysians against mistaking social media snippets for knowledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that children are losing the habit of reading as social media shortens attention spans and replaces knowledge with bite-sized information. Launching the 2026 National Book Fair at the World Trade Centre here, Anwar who professed himself a bookworm, urged schools, teachers and parents to reignite interest in reading to build a more informed society. “The reading culture has become a problem in the modern world,” he said.

items.title: Storm warning out for Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan, Johor, Sarawak, Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for seven states today, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to persist until 7.00 pm. Central and southern states are also affected, specifically Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin in Negeri Sembilan, alongside Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, and Kota Tinggi in Johor. In East Malaysia, MetMalaysia has flagged several divisions in Sarawak, namely Sibu, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu (Tatau), and Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri, and Marudi).

items.title: Missing witness sinks case against two men accused of possessing 150kg of drugs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 29 — Two former steamboat restaurant workers were acquitted and freed by the High Court here today after a judge ruled the prosecution failed to secure a key witness sought by the defence in a case involving nearly 150kg of methamphetamine and heroin. Chan Shi Wei, 29, and Lim Chan Hau, 30, had spent six years in remand over the charges linked to a house in Kota Damansara. Bhupindar also found the prosecution failed to effectively challenge key aspects of the defence during cross-examination.

items.title: RM24m Bukit Jalil stadium upgrade project under MACC scrutiny after complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today confirmed it is reviewing complaints linked to the evaluation process and upgrade works at the National Stadium Bukit Jalil. It said it is examining related information and documents before deciding on further investigation, Berita Harian reported this afternoon. “MACC always takes seriously any allegations involving corruption, abuse of power and misconduct affecting public interest and government procurement,” it was quoted as saying.
